REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (3867-3869 17th Street, San Francisco, Ca 94114 (Apn: 3581-073))
Off Calendar Jul-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Matthew Woodbury.

Mr. Siracusa announced that there was a slight error in the MLS listing, that it indicated the monthly rent at $2,298.00 and that the correct monthly rent amount is $2,287.00. The sale was confirmed at $1,680,500.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed June 16, 2017. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $1,730,000.00 is required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-17-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Matthew Woodbury (Trustee).

Ms. Alavi stated that the Settlor passed away a while back, that there is a parcel of property in San Francisco that they have been trying to sell, that they are having difficulty selling it and that they got an offer yesterday which they may accept. Ms. Alavi also stated that they were trying to make this a final accounting, but will not be able to do so, that some of the Beneficiaries are in Peru and asked for time to do an interim accounting. Status on filing of the Third and Final Account is continued to June 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an interim accounting.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Tim Kneis. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-13-296405 Conservatorship of Merel & PTR-13-296910 Merel Trust.
Appearances: Avid Alavi for Matthew Woodbury.

The Court stated that the estate is invested in Mutual Funds, asked if it was necessary to continue to pay the 1% annually to an investment advisor, that there should be a way to avoid the drain on the estate and suggested a periodic review at an hourly rate. Ms. Alavi stated that she will speak with the investment advisor to see if there is an alternative solution, that she will look into it and address it with the second accounting, addressed the high medication cost stating that coverage is reduced because it is not service related, that Mr. Woodbury has applied for Medicare D in hopes of reducing the costs, that Mr. Woodbury is actively involved in managing the properties, that he has obtained maximum rent for the small studio, has been able to rent the garage and that Ms. Alavi believes he is keeping time sheets. The Court suggested Ms. Alavi look at the time sheets as the court will want justification of time fees going forward. The Court granted the petition for accounting (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
